jokes.text: Hey! Did you know that everybody here went out last night without us? Oh, nerds!
jokes.setup: Liz discovers her staff went out without her
jokes.punchline: Her immediate response is to call them 'nerds' - the reverse of typical social dynamics
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's social awkwardness - she responds to exclusion by name-calling like a child

jokes.text: Nerds?
jokes.setup: Liz just called everyone nerds
jokes.punchline: Tracy's confused reaction to this odd insult choice
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat that sells the absurdity of Liz's response. Economy of one word.

jokes.text: Well, I yelled 'baba booey' at Walter Cronkite's funeral, so I actually have no idea of what's rude or not.
jokes.setup: Tracy is asked about rudeness
jokes.punchline: He admits to yelling a Howard Stern reference at a revered journalist's funeral
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy line - perfectly captures his complete disconnect from social norms with a specific, memorable absurd detail

jokes.text: Could you take care of Angie like a husband until this whole ass-ache blows over?
jokes.setup: Tracy needs someone to help with his pregnant wife
jokes.punchline: He calls pregnancy complications an 'ass-ache' instead of a hassle
jokes.explanation: Tracy's malapropism shows his self-absorption and verbal creativity

jokes.text: I haven't seen your brow that furrowed since you saw that picture of Helen Mirren in a bikini. How is it possible? Is she a wizard?
jokes.setup: Jack looks troubled
jokes.punchline: Liz compares it to his confusion over Helen Mirren's attractiveness, then asks if she's a wizard
jokes.explanation: Classic 30 Rock - starts with relatable observation then escalates to absurd magical explanation

jokes.text: Oh, Nan... Na. What? I was saying, 'oh, Nana.' Have I not told you about my grandmother fetish?
jokes.setup: Jack almost says Nancy's name during sex
jokes.punchline: He covers by claiming he has a grandmother fetish
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment - his ability to improvise increasingly disturbing lies to cover social awkwardness

jokes.text: Avery actually tried to get on board. Later that night, she put some mothballs behind her ears and fed me peppermints.
jokes.setup: Jack's cover story about grandmother fetish
jokes.punchline: Avery actually role-played it with specific old lady details
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity perfectly - Avery's commitment to the bit with specific sensory details is brilliant

jokes.text: I've had to make some tough calls over the years, switching Sheinhardt's wig production to 100% Chinese cadaver hair, turning down Dick Cheney's offer to become king of Iraq
jokes.setup: Jack lists his tough business decisions
jokes.punchline: Includes using corpse hair for wigs and refusing to rule Iraq
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack backstory - casually mentions horrifying business practices and geopolitical power plays

jokes.text: [NBC chime plays] it's fresh.
jokes.setup: Jack mentions selecting NBC's new logo
jokes.punchline: The actual NBC chime plays followed by weak slogan
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about NBC's actual rebranding efforts - the underwhelming 'it's fresh' tagline

jokes.text: Although, sometimes when you try so hard to find love, you can't see that it's been standing in front of you the whole time. Oh, good God.
jokes.setup: Liz gives romantic advice to Jack
jokes.punchline: She realizes mid-sentence this sounds like she's hitting on him and reacts with horror
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment where Liz catches herself delivering rom-com dialogue

jokes.text: No! I don't care if you're safe! I love you!
jokes.setup: Kenneth offers to call when he arrives safely
jokes.punchline: Jack's emphatic response sounds romantic but he's just being a bad boss
jokes.explanation: Subverts expectation - sounds like declaration of love but is actually Jack being callously professional

jokes.text: Oh, I dropped my glass! [crash]
jokes.setup: Liz tries to pour herself a drink to seem sophisticated
jokes.punchline: Immediately drops and breaks the glass
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy - fits Liz's character but not particularly original or clever

jokes.text: Is that supposed to be a broom? Anchor the handle.
jokes.setup: Liz tries to clean up broken glass
jokes.punchline: Her makeshift broom is so pathetic Jack has to give cleaning instructions
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing both Liz's incompetence and Jack's need to manage everything

jokes.text: To be honest, I couldn't really understand anything Rick James was saying.
jokes.setup: Kenneth asks if Tracy remembers his wedding vows
jokes.punchline: Tracy had Rick James officiate his wedding
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy backstory - completely absurd but totally believable for his character

jokes.text: And once I'm there, I'll determine how much to charge you.
jokes.setup: Tracy tells Kenneth to help himself to food in the fridge
jokes.punchline: Then adds he'll charge Kenneth for whatever he eats
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy moment - appears generous then reveals his selfish nature

jokes.text: Oh, God, it looks like the underside of an octopus!
jokes.setup: Liz asks Frank about his doctor's appointment
jokes.punchline: He describes something medical with a disturbing sea creature comparison
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor with vivid imagery - typical Frank oversharing

jokes.text: Hey, Liz, can I talk to you and Jenna in... The office where everyone clips their toenails? Not cool, guys.
jokes.setup: Cerie wants to talk privately
jokes.punchline: She reveals the office has a designated toenail clipping room
jokes.explanation: Gross workplace detail that's unfortunately believable

jokes.text: Well, it was off for a while, because my fiancé was on his dad's yacht and got captured by pirates, but now, thanks to the A-Team, he's back.
jokes.setup: Cerie explains why her wedding was delayed
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of pirate kidnapping and 80s TV team rescue
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating absurdity delivered matter-of-factly - pirates and fictional TV team treated as normal

jokes.text: The bridesmaids are you guys, Andy Roddick's wife, my Dutch cousins, and Penelope Cruz's hotter sister Monica.
jokes.setup: Cerie lists her bridesmaids
jokes.punchline: Mix of normal people and celebrities, plus claiming someone is hotter than Penelope Cruz
jokes.explanation: Good Cerie moment - her casual name-dropping and impossible beauty standards

jokes.text: You're gonna be so out of place, Liz.
jokes.setup: List of glamorous bridesmaids
jokes.punchline: Jenna points out Liz won't fit in
jokes.explanation: Typical Jenna meanness - not particularly clever but fits character

jokes.text: Now that Aris is finally getting over his Stockholm syndrome.
jokes.setup: Cerie mentions her wedding schedule filling up
jokes.punchline: Casually references her fiancé having Stockholm syndrome from pirate capture
jokes.explanation: Dark callback that adds psychological trauma to the absurd pirate story

jokes.text: You guys may have to walk down the aisle with some pirates.
jokes.setup: Wedding planning continues
jokes.punchline: The pirates who kidnapped her fiancé are now wedding guests
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd escalation - the criminals become wedding party members

jokes.text: I'm doing a juice fast, and it's making me really grouchy. Aah!
jokes.setup: Jenna explains why she can't plan the party
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates the grouchiness with an angry outburst
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy bit - not particularly clever but serviceable

jokes.text: [rapping] * and they're ain't no party * like a Liz Lemon party * 'cause a Liz Lemon party is mandatory *
jokes.setup: Liz declares she's throwing a party
jokes.punchline: Raps a parody of Coolio's song but makes it about mandatory attendance
jokes.explanation: Good parody that perfectly captures Liz's awkward attempts to be cool and her managerial authoritarianism

jokes.text: I apologize for letting a Kashmiri into your office.
jokes.setup: Jonathan apologizes for the janitor interruption
jokes.punchline: He makes it about ethnicity rather than professional disruption
jokes.explanation: Dark character moment showing Jonathan's casual prejudice

jokes.text: Every April 22nd, I honor Richard Nixon's death by getting drunk and making some unpopular decisions.
jokes.setup: Jack explains why he signed the janitor contract
jokes.punchline: He has an annual tradition of honoring Nixon by making bad choices
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jack line - perfectly captures his Republican politics and tendency toward self-destructive rituals

jokes.text: Ever since I am a little boy, I am dreaming of hosing the tonight shift.
jokes.setup: Khonani explains his career ambitions
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'hosting' as 'hosing' which changes the meaning completely
jokes.explanation: Good malapropism that accidentally creates a crude double entendre

jokes.text: [NBC chime plays] the biggest loser network.
jokes.setup: Jack mentions NBC again
jokes.punchline: This time the chime is followed by a self-aware insult
jokes.explanation: Follow-up to earlier NBC joke - less fresh but good self-deprecation

jokes.text: No, I am not. I am 'innoventing.' A word that I just innovented.
jokes.setup: Accused of making things up
jokes.punchline: Jack invents a word for inventing words, demonstrating the behavior he's denying
jokes.explanation: Clever recursive joke - Jack's corporate speak tendency taken to logical extreme

jokes.text: I trained him to hate white people, because, not to profile, but most ghosts are white.
jokes.setup: Tracy explains why his dog attacks Kenneth
jokes.punchline: He trained the dog to be racist against ghosts, which are mostly white
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy logic - combines racism, supernatural beliefs, and statistical analysis in completely absurd way. Instant classic.

jokes.text: He gets shocked if he tries to leave the property, just because he'd run away and cause car accidents and impregnate neighbors' horses.
jokes.setup: Tracy explains the dog's invisible fence
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly ridiculous reasons why the dog needed to be contained
jokes.explanation: Great escalating list that goes from plausible to completely absurd with horse bestiality

jokes.text: No, we're not supposed to do any sex stuff while she's on bed rest. Oh! Like a real one! I'm on it!
jokes.setup: Kenneth mentions Angie wants a peanut butter and jelly sandwich
jokes.punchline: Tracy initially thinks it's sexual slang, then realizes it's actual food
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Tracy's oversexualized mindset and basic incompetence

jokes.text: Nope! I'm in a strip club! My bad!
jokes.setup: Tracy was supposed to make a sandwich
jokes.punchline: He immediately ended up at a strip club instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy moment - his complete inability to focus on simple tasks

jokes.text: Oh, you mean frozen water? I don't know.
jokes.setup: Liz asks about ice for the party
jokes.punchline: Jenna initially thinks she means the drug, then admits ignorance about regular ice
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna moment showing her industry experience with drugs but cluelessness about normal things

jokes.text: And that Black Eyed Peas song they wrote for bar mitzvahs.
jokes.setup: Liz lists party entertainment
jokes.punchline: Claims a pop song was specifically written for Jewish coming-of-age ceremonies
jokes.explanation: Funny observation about generic party music being oddly specific to celebrations

jokes.text: No, it's not that. We're dating.
jokes.setup: Jack assumes Liz calls the janitor for toilet problems
jokes.punchline: She reveals they're in a romantic relationship
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz romantic situation - dating inappropriate people in awkward ways

jokes.text: Honestly, these immigrants have a tough life, no health care, and I kind of just thought he'd die before it became a thing.
jokes.setup: Jack explains why he made the promise to Khonani
jokes.punchline: Casually admits he expected the janitor to die from lack of healthcare
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly Jack - his casual cruelty disguised as corporate pragmatism

jokes.text: He collects classic car... [coughing] Cardboard. Classic cardboard.
jokes.setup: Khonani describes Subhas's hobbies
jokes.punchline: Catches himself mid-lie and makes it even more pathetic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Khonani's attempt to seem dignified while being petty

jokes.text: I'm like a chameleon, always a lizard!
jokes.setup: Tracy says he can't change
jokes.punchline: Uses chameleon metaphor incorrectly - they change but are always lizards
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy line - accidentally profound wisdom through complete misunderstanding of metaphors

jokes.text: First of all, the secret service never gave me back my t-shirt cannon
jokes.setup: Tracy explains why he can't recreate inaugural ball behavior
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions having a t-shirt cannon confiscated by federal agents
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy backstory - completely absurd but delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: People don't say that anymore. They say 'surf party, usa.'
jokes.setup: Liz says her party will be 'off the hook'
jokes.punchline: Tracy claims people now say an equally dated/nonsensical phrase
jokes.explanation: Good absurd Tracy moment - his complete disconnect from current slang

jokes.text: But parties are like frisbees. If you throw them the wrong way, they'll veer off in a bad direction, and then your kid will fall into a quarry.
jokes.setup: Tracy gives Liz advice about parties
jokes.punchline: Compares parties to frisbees in increasingly disturbing way ending in child death
jokes.explanation: Excellent Tracy metaphor that starts reasonable then goes completely off the rails into dark territory

jokes.text: It will turn on you, like your wife after your kid has fallen into a quarry.
jokes.setup: Tracy continues the party advice
jokes.punchline: Extends the quarry metaphor to marital breakdown
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up that doubles down on the absurd darkness

jokes.text: It will turn on you, like your wife after your kid has fallen into a quarry.
jokes.setup: Continuing party advice
jokes.punchline: Extends the quarry metaphor to marriage consequences
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends the absurd metaphor. Not quite as strong as the original but solid follow-up.

jokes.text: But my body's gonna take me to Liz Lemon's.
jokes.setup: Tracy is torn about staying home vs going to party
jokes.punchline: Describes his impulses as bodily possession
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy logic - treats his lack of self-control as external force

jokes.text: Put the electric dog collar on me. It would be my honor.
jokes.setup: Tracy needing to stay home but lacking self-control
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's eager willingness to collar his boss
jokes.explanation: Good absurd solution with perfect Kenneth response. His enthusiasm for the task is very in character.

jokes.text: No, I am not. I am 'innoventing.' A word that I just innovented.
jokes.setup: Explaining his solution to the janitor problem
jokes.punchline: Uses his made-up word again
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier joke - less impact on repeat

jokes.text: [NBC chime plays] It's fresh.
jokes.setup: Jack mentions NBC again
jokes.punchline: Third iteration of the NBC chime joke
jokes.explanation: Third use of same joke structure - diminishing returns

jokes.text: That's how my kids keep me out of the liquor cabinet.
jokes.setup: Tracy explains using glue in a lock
jokes.punchline: Reveals his children have to prevent his alcoholism
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about child responsibility for parent's addiction - perfectly Tracy

jokes.text: Unfortunately, there's only one Tracy Jordan, but sometimes he's needed in two places.
jokes.setup: Tracy needs to be home and at the party
jokes.punchline: Treats himself as a limited resource in third person
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy narcissism - talking about himself as precious commodity

jokes.text: Wait. I don't walk that well.
jokes.setup: Kenneth is told to be Tracy
jokes.punchline: Identifies walking as his main impediment to impersonation
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth moment - his literal-minded approach to impossible task

jokes.text: There's no trash, Jack. Ever since you moved Subhas to 10:00, he takes all the trash. No trash left for Khonani.
jokes.setup: Jack asks how Khonani's first day is going
jokes.punchline: Jack's solution created a problem - there's no work left for Khonani
jokes.explanation: Good ironic consequence of Jack's corporate solution creating new problems

jokes.text: And with Nancy and Avery, I'm the trash shift.
jokes.setup: Jack realizes parallel between janitor situation and his love life
jokes.punchline: Compares himself to the thing both women want - the trash shift
jokes.explanation: Good metaphorical connection - Jack realizes he's like garbage in the comparison

jokes.text: I have a job for me at fox... [coughing] Woods. Foxwoods casino. My cousin is head janitor there.
jokes.setup: Khonani says he has another job lined up
jokes.punchline: Starts to say Fox network, corrects to casino, reveals nepotism
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and character moment showing Khonani's pretensions and family connections

jokes.text: I suggest you cut off a finger and throw it in the river.
jokes.setup: Kenneth is being hunted by Tracy's dog
jokes.punchline: Tracy suggests extreme body mutilation as casual solution
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Tracy advice - treating extreme violence as practical solution

jokes.text: This looks like the post-coital suite at the NBA All-Star game.
jokes.setup: Jenna sees Liz's party setup
jokes.punchline: Compares it to a very specific sexual scenario from sports
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna line - her familiarity with celebrity sexual situations

jokes.text: Juice fast delirium, Liz.
jokes.setup: Jenna does something weird with the microphone
jokes.punchline: Explains her behavior as diet-induced psychosis
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier juice fast setup - serviceable but not brilliant

jokes.text: I deserve to be loved! [...] I am a proud, single woman!
jokes.setup: Party goes wrong, Liz has public breakdown
jokes.punchline: Declares her worth and singleness to horrified co-workers
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Liz's desperation and social awkwardness

jokes.text: You think when I was a kid, I dreamed of someday paying $1,200 for a karaoke machine to impress a bunch of pasty losers? And a professional singer, who's beautiful, but doesn't know it.
jokes.setup: Liz continues her breakdown
jokes.punchline: Insults her co-workers, then backhanded compliment to Jenna
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Liz's anger mixed with reluctant affection for Jenna

jokes.text: [whimpering] I had nowhere else to go! [dog barking] Dog! Dog attack! [screaming]
jokes.setup: Kenneth arrives at party being chased
jokes.punchline: Tracy's racist ghost-hunting dog attacks the party
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to earlier dog setup - physical comedy chaos

jokes.text: Maybe this doesn't work on people. Aah! Aah! Oh, my neck! My swan-like neck!
jokes.setup: Tracy tests if the dog collar affects humans
jokes.punchline: Gets shocked, specifically worried about his beautiful neck
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Tracy's vanity about his neck

jokes.text: I'm free. I can go anywhere I want... Like Liz's party or one of those places where you skydive over a huge fan.
jokes.setup: Tracy escapes the electric fence
jokes.punchline: Lists normal option then completely bizarre activity
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy line - starts normal then goes completely absurd

jokes.text: That's just a cardboard cutout of her.
jokes.setup: Someone mentions Cerie didn't come to the party
jokes.punchline: Reveals Liz used a cardboard cutout as decoration
jokes.explanation: Good reveal about Liz's desperate party planning

jokes.text: Yes, that's exactly why we like you. We love you. You solve our problems. That's what you're good at.
jokes.setup: Liz asks if they only like her when they need help
jokes.punchline: Frank confirms this completely without sugar-coating
jokes.explanation: Good honest moment - Frank's blunt assessment of their relationship with Liz

jokes.text: Yeah. You know who's fun at parties? Paris Hilton, Andy Dick, Tracy, and they're all gonna burn in hell.
jokes.setup: Discussing who's fun at parties
jokes.punchline: Lists party people then adds religious condemnation
jokes.explanation: Good Lutz line showing his judgmental religious side mixed with pop culture knowledge

jokes.text: You know who's fun at parties? Paris Hilton, Andy Dick, Tracy, and they're all gonna burn in hell.
jokes.setup: Defending Liz not being fun
jokes.punchline: Lists party people then condemns them to hell
jokes.explanation: Great Lutz religious judgment mixed with support for Liz. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: If you deliver me from this, I promise every Sunday for the rest of my life, I'll go to... Huh? I'm through it. Pizza Hut. I'll go to Pizza Hut.
jokes.setup: Liz makes a desperate religious bargain
jokes.punchline: Starts to promise church, realizes she's safe, switches to Pizza Hut
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's religious hypocrisy and food obsession

jokes.text: You want mommy to make the monsters go away.
jokes.setup: Frank explains their relationship with Liz
jokes.punchline: Describes her as mommy protecting them from monsters
jokes.explanation: Good insight into their dysfunctional work family dynamic

jokes.text: No! You're not book! Wanting to be book is not book.
jokes.setup: Liz offers Subhas a headshot for his book
jokes.punchline: Subhas explains the zen-like philosophy of being 'book'
jokes.explanation: Good character payoff - Subhas's mysterious photo book philosophy

jokes.text: Top gun high-five for courage? Only because you look like you need it so badly.
jokes.setup: Jack offers a Top Gun high-five
jokes.punchline: Liz agrees but only out of pity
jokes.explanation: Good ending moment showing Liz's reluctant affection for Jack
